title: Caramelised Onion Tart
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Paul Hollywood-style caramelised onion tart: buttery pastry, slow onions and a savoury custard for TM6 and TM7.
intro: Paul Hollywood's savoury baking has that proper British comfort: crisp pastry, deep onion sweetness and no fuss. The Thermomix makes the shortcrust and slowly softens the onions; the oven does the blind-bake and final set.
ingredients: 80 g mature cheddar or gruyere, grated
steps: Place flour, cold butter and salt in the mixing bowl. Mix 10 sec / 0°C / speed 6 until sandy.
Add chilled water and mix 8 sec / 0°C / speed 4, just until the dough clumps. Tip out, press into a disc, wrap and chill 20 minutes.
Roll pastry into a 23 cm tart tin. Blind-bake in a preheated oven 15 min / 190°C / no speed with weights, then 5 min / 190°C / no speed without weights. The Thermomix is not used for this oven step.

Place sliced onions, olive oil, extra butter, sugar and balsamic vinegar in the mixing bowl. Cook 25 min / 120°C / Reverse / speed 1, MC off with the simmering basket on the lid, until soft and golden. Cool 10 minutes.
In the clean bowl, mix eggs, cream, cheese, thyme and pepper 12 sec / 0°C / speed 3.
Spread onions into the tart shell, pour over the custard and bake in the oven 25–30 min / 180°C / no speed until set and burnished. Rest 10 min / 0°C / no speed before slicing.
tips: Do not skip chilling the pastry; it keeps the tart shell from shrinking.
MC off is important while caramelising onions so moisture can escape.
Let the onions cool slightly before adding the custard so the eggs do not scramble.

faq.q: Can the Thermomix make tart pastry?
faq.a: Yes. It rubs butter into flour quickly, but stop as soon as the dough clumps so the pastry stays short and crisp.

faq.q: Do I still need an oven for onion tart?
faq.a: Yes. The Thermomix makes the pastry, onions and custard, but the tart shell and final tart need baking in the oven.

faq.q: Is this the original Paul Hollywood recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Paul Hollywood and onion tart; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
